steps.title: Identify the text's structure
steps.content: The passage walks through a mechanism step by step: excess power pumps water up to a summit lake during low demand, and that stored water drains back down to generate electricity during peak demand. The text is a factual, sequential explanation of how one specific technology operates.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — To point out the differences between two methods of energy generation: The text never compares pumped-storage hydropower to any other method; nuclear plants are only mentioned as the power source for pumping. ✗
  • Choice B — To explain the basics of how a specific energy technology works: This matches exactly—the passage explains, step by step, how Cedar Mountain’s pumped-storage system works. ✓
  • Choice C — To encourage regional electric utilities to build energy storage facilities: The text contains no persuasive language and makes no recommendation to build more facilities. ✗
  • Choice D — To discuss the benefits of a new energy technology: Cedar Mountain was built in the 1970s, so the technology is not new, and the text does not frame itself as a discussion of benefits. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
